tool.name: query_cve_database
tool.description:
Query the NVD (National Vulnerability Database) for CVEs affecting a technology.
Use this tool when you: - Identify a technology/version and want to find known vulnerabilities - Need to check if a specific version is vulnerable - Want to find CVEs for bug bounty testing
Examples: - Found "nginx/1.18.0" → Query for nginx CVEs affecting 1.18.0 - Detected WordPress 5.9 → Find WordPress vulnerabilities - Identified Apache Tomcat → Check for recent Apache CVEs

parameters.parameter: Specific version to check (e.g., "1.18.0", "5.9.3"). Results will be filtered to only include CVEs that affect this version.
Minimum CVSS severity to include. Options: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL. Default: LOW (includes all severities)
Maximum number of CVEs to return. Default: 20

tool.returns: Dictionary containing: - success: Boolean indicating if query succeeded - cves: List of CVE entries with severity, description, references, exploit availability - total_found: Number of matching CVEs - recommendations: Testing suggestions based on findings
tool.usage_example:
<!-- Find nginx 1.18.0 vulnerabilities --> <function name="query_cve_database"> <parameter name="keyword">nginx</parameter> <parameter name="version">1.18.0</parameter> <parameter name="severity_threshold">MEDIUM</parameter> </function>
<!-- Find all critical WordPress CVEs --> <function name="query_cve_database"> <parameter name="keyword">wordpress</parameter> <parameter name="severity_threshold">CRITICAL</parameter> </function>

tool.name: search_exploits
tool.description:
Search Exploit-DB for exploits and Proof-of-Concept code.
Use this tool when you: - Found a CVE and need working exploit code - Want to find PoCs for a vulnerability type - Need exploit code for a specific technology
The tool searches Exploit-DB and falls back to GitHub for additional results.

parameters.parameter: Search term - can be CVE ID, product name, or vulnerability type. Examples: "CVE-2021-23017", "wordpress plugin sqli", "nginx buffer overflow"

tool.returns: Dictionary containing: - success: Boolean indicating if search succeeded - exploits: List with exploit IDs, titles, URLs, and download links - alternative_searches: Fallback search URLs if results are limited
tool.usage_example:
<!-- Find exploits for CVE-2021-23017 --> <function name="search_exploits"> <parameter name="search_term">CVE-2021-23017</parameter> </function>
<!-- Find WordPress SQL injection exploits --> <function name="search_exploits"> <parameter name="search_term">wordpress sqli</parameter> <parameter name="exploit_type">webapps</parameter> </function>

tool.name: get_cve_details
tool.description:
Get comprehensive details about a specific CVE.
Use this tool when you: - Need full details about a vulnerability - Want to understand the attack vector and complexity - Need to find patches and mitigations - Want exploit and PoC references
tool.returns: Dictionary containing: - cve_details: Full description, CVSS scores, attack vector details - affected_products: List of affected software/versions - exploitability: Exploit and PoC availability info - remediation: Patch links and mitigation recommendations

tool.name: search_github_advisories
tool.description:
Search GitHub Security Advisories database for package vulnerabilities.
Use this tool when you: - Testing applications using npm, pip, Maven, or other package managers - Want to find vulnerabilities in specific packages - Need to check for package ecosystem-specific issues
tool.returns: Dictionary containing: - advisories: List of GitHub security advisories with CVE links - vulnerable_versions: Affected version ranges - patched_versions: Fixed versions information
tool.usage_example:
<!-- Find npm package vulnerabilities --> <function name="search_github_advisories"> <parameter name="ecosystem">npm</parameter> <parameter name="severity">critical</parameter> </function>
<!-- Check specific package --> <function name="search_github_advisories"> <parameter name="package">lodash</parameter> <parameter name="keyword">prototype pollution</parameter> </function>

tool.name: get_technology_vulnerabilities
tool.description:
Get comprehensive vulnerability information for a technology from multiple sources.
This is a HIGH-LEVEL aggregation tool that queries NVD, Exploit-DB, and GitHub to provide a complete picture of known vulnerabilities for a technology.
Use this tool when you: - Identified a technology during reconnaissance - Want all known vulns and exploits in one query - Need prioritized testing recommendations

parameters.parameter: Comma-separated vulnerability types to focus on. Options: "rce,sqli,xss,ssrf,lfi,xxe,auth,dos"
Whether to search for available exploits. Default: true

tool.returns: Dictionary containing: - vulnerabilities: Aggregated list from all sources - exploits_available: Known exploits with download links - attack_surface: Potential attack vectors identified - testing_priority: Prioritized list of what to test first - summary: Statistics about findings
tool.usage_example:
<!-- Get all nginx 1.18.0 vulnerabilities with exploit focus --> <function name="get_technology_vulnerabilities"> <parameter name="technology">nginx</parameter> <parameter name="version">1.18.0</parameter> <parameter name="include_exploits">true</parameter> </function>
<!-- Focus on RCE and SQLi for WordPress --> <function name="get_technology_vulnerabilities"> <parameter name="technology">wordpress</parameter> <parameter name="vuln_types">rce,sqli,xss</parameter> </function>

tool.name: search_packetstorm
tool.description:
Search PacketStorm Security for exploits, tools, and security papers.
PacketStorm often has exploits and tools not found on other databases. Use this as a supplementary search source.

parameters.parameter: Search term (CVE ID, product name, vulnerability type)

tool.returns: Dictionary containing: - results: List of matching content with URLs - direct_search_url: URL for manual browsing
